title: Use a (readonly) container image as a volume source
body:
### Feature request description

This is somewhat related to https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5837 but a different approach.

I'd like some sort of a syntax for mounting a container image directly into a running container.  This functionality is already available from Containerfile but not from `podman run`:

If you have a build stage called `base`, then you can do something like this, for example:

```
RUN --mount=type=bind,from=base,target=/mnt/base <<EOF
```

and you'll get the raw image at `/mnt/base`.

It would be lovely to have an equivalent functionality like

`podman run --volume-from-image=image-name:/:/mnt/base` or something like that.

You can already kinda sorta do this yourself with something like:

`podman run -v"$(podman unshare podman image mount image-name)":/mnt/base`

The use I have in mind right now for this particular feature is so that bootc containers can gain access to their own (raw) filesystem contents without the various bind mounts for things like /etc/resolv.conf... but it seems like it would be useful as a general feature for use on other container images as well.

For the "use my own root fs" case I tried a few tricks: 
 - mount -o bind / /mnt
 - `open_tree("/", OPEN_TREE_CLONE)` and inspecting the content via the passed fd

but in both cases I still see the overrides from the container runtime...

Thanks!

comments.body: Another form that this could take is that, in privileged mode, the container's unmodified root filesystem image simply always gets mounted at `/run/.containerimg/` or so.
Sorry.  I should have read more closely.  I was looking under `--volume` and related options but this is already available in `--mount=type=image,...`.
